content.problemStatement:
Chef is ordering food online (instead of cooking) and the bill comes out to be Rs. XXX. Chef can use one of the following two coupons to avail a discount.


Get 101010 percent off on the bill amount
Get a flat discount of Rs. 100100100 on the bill amount
What is the maximum discount Chef can avail?


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer XXX - the bill amount before discount.


Output Format
For each testcase, output the maximum discount Chef can avail.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer X, the bill amount (a multiple of 100).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the maximum discount Chef can obtain.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: 10% of 300 = 30, flat discount = 100 → maximum is 100.\nTest case 2: 10% of 1300 = 130, flat discount = 100 → maximum is 130.\nTest case 3: 10% of 1000 = 100, flat discount = 100 → both equal, answer is 100.

solution.approach: For each bill amount X we have two possible discounts: 1) 10% of X, which equals X/10 because X is a multiple of 100, and 2) a flat Rs.100 discount. The optimal discount is simply the larger of these two values. The algorithm iterates over all test cases, computes both discounts, and prints the maximum. This runs in O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work per test case.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used.

code.Python3:
import sys

def main():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0])
    out = []
    idx = 1
    for _ in range(t):
        x = int(data[idx]); idx += 1
        d1 = x // 10  # 10% discount
        d2 = 100      # flat discount
        out.append(str(max(d1, d2)))
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
